data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: NVDA, TRV, ASML, UAL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures slipped on Wednesday as the Trump administration intensified scrutiny of critical mineral imports, adding to existing probes into pharmaceuticals and semiconductors and fueling renewed trade tension concerns. Meanwhile, March retail sales data showed resilience, with core retail sales beating expectations and the headline figure coming in line with consensus.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) +6% – Shares rose after the carrier posted better-than-expected Q1 earnings of $0.91 per share, swinging from a loss of $0.15 a year ago and topping estimates by $0.17. Growth in its basic economy segment helped drive the beat. Capacity rose 4.9% Y/Y, while TRASM increased 0.5% and CASM dropped 3.4%. Excluding fuel, CASM rose 0.3%. International revenue climbed 5.2%, offsetting a 3.9% drop in domestic revenue. However, management warned that a recession could significantly dent profitability. For Q2, United forecasts EPS of $3.25 to $4.25, bracketing the $3.97 consensus. FY2025 guidance ranges from $11.50–$13.50 per share in a stable economy to $7.00–$9.00 in a recession, vs. the consensus of $10.36 per share.
  • The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) +4% – Shares climbed after the insurer raised its quarterly cash dividend by 4.8% to $1.10 per share and posted Q1 earnings supported by strong underlying underwriting income despite a surge in catastrophe losses, primarily from January's California wildfires. The combined ratio rose to 102.5% from 83.2% in Q4 and 93.9% in the year-ago quarter. Meanwhile, the underlying combined ratio improved to 84.8%, up from 84.0% in the prior quarter and down from 87.7% a year earlier.

Biggest stock losers

  • Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) -6% – Shares declined after the company said it received notice from the U.S. government requiring a special license to export its H20 GPUs to China indefinitely, raising fresh concerns over trade restrictions. The company expects to take a $5.5B charge in its upcoming quarterly results due to the development. The H20 chip, designed to comply with earlier export rules, had seen over $16B in orders from Chinese firms like Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in early 2025 ahead of the anticipated ban.
  • ASML Holding (NASDAQ:ASML) -5% – Shares dropped after the Dutch semiconductor equipment maker reported mixed Q1 results and missed order expectations and flagged potential demand headwinds from new U.S. trade restrictions. Net bookings totaled €3.94B ($4.47B) in Q1 2025, falling short of analysts’ forecast of €4.89B. CEO Christophe Fouquet noted that while the long-term demand outlook remains strong, particularly with AI as a key driver, “uncertainty with some of our customers” could push revenue toward the lower end of ASML’s 2025 forecast range of €30B–€35B.
  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) -6% – The stock fell as investors looked past upbeat Q1 results and focused on declining revenue and margins. Operating revenue dipped 1% Y/Y to $2.92B, dragged down by a 5% reduction in average truck count in Dedicated Contract Services, 15% fewer stops in Final Mile Services, and a 13% drop in load volumes in Integrated Capacity Solutions. Operating income slid 8% to $178.7M, weighed by lower DCS revenue, weaker yields in JBI, rising insurance and medical costs, and higher equipment and maintenance expenses.
  • Interactive Brokers Group (NASDAQ:IBKR) -9% – Shares slumped after the company delivered mixed Q1 results, with earnings falling short of expectations despite a 28% dividend hike and the announcement of a 4-for-1 stock split. The profit miss appeared to overshadow the shareholder-friendly moves, weighing on investor sentiment. The stock split will take effect after market close on June 17, with trading on a split-adjusted basis beginning June 18.
  • Kingsoft Cloud (NASDAQ:KC) -16% – Shares tumbled after the Chinese cloud services provider announced a public offering of 18.5M American depositary shares (ADS), each representing 15 ordinary shares, totaling 277.5M ordinary shares. Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase additional ADSs. In a concurrent private placement, major shareholder Kingsoft Corporation (OTCPK:KSFTF) agreed to buy additional shares at the public offering price. The company plans to use the net proceeds to upgrade and expand its infrastructure, invest in technology and product development, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow fall as Nvidia's China licensing requirement fuels trade war concerns
data.description:
Major averages were lower on Wednesday, as worries about a U.S.-China trade war rose on news that Nvidia will need a special license to ship graphic processing units to China.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 5 basis points to 3.80%.

The core retail sales beat consensus in March, while the headline number came in line

Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs.

"After a strong start to the week, the market mood turned more negative again yesterday, as tensions between the US and China showed signs of further escalation," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"So after a brief period of greater stability in markets, that reminded investors about the ongoing risks of escalation, raising fears that the trade war could still get worse from here," Allen added.

Nvidia (NVDA) fell 6.5% as it received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

"Some politicians may believe trade is more important than it is. Trade does matter economically, but a 'made in China' product in a store does not mean all the price paid goes to China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

President Donald Trump has also directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

Also on the economic side, industrial production dipped slightly more than expected in March to 0.3% M/M, weaker than the -0.2% consensus figure.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also expected to speak later in the day.

data.title: Valero set to shut Benicia refinery in California, books $1.1B charge
data.description:
Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO) said Wednesday it has submitted notice to the California Energy Commission that it plans to idle, restructure, or cease refining operations at its Benicia refinery by April 2026, while it evaluates strategic alternatives for its remaining operations in California.

Valero (NYSE:VLO) said it recorded a combined $1.1B pre-tax impairment charge for the Benicia and Wilmington refineries in the state, which it expects to be treated as a special item and excluded from Q1 adjusted earnings; the amount also included the recognition of expected asset retirement obligations of $337M as of March 31.

California regulators slapped Valero (VLO) with a record-breaking $82M fine last year for failing to report excess toxic emissions from the Benicia refinery that occurred during 2003-19 and were not disclosed to the public until 2022.

The Benicia city council has planned to vote on a controversial industrial safety ordinance which would create a citizens oversight commission, boost community air quality monitoring and empower city officials to issue fines for safety and air quality violations; Valero (VLO) has threatened to sue the city if it moves ahead with the ordinance.

data.title: Lyft gains with the FreeNow acquisition seen opening up market share opportunities in Europe
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) pushed higher in early trading on Wednesday after the company announced the acquisition of FreeNow earlier in the day.

FreeNow is a leading European mobility service provider headquartered in Hamburg, Germany. Founded in 2019 as a joint venture between BMW Group (OTC:BMWKY) and Mercedes-Benz Mobility (OTCPK:MBGAF), FreeNow operates as a multi-mobility app with a strong focus on taxi services, but also offers a wide range of urban transport options, including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, eScooters, eBikes, and eMopeds. FreeNow operates in over 150 cities across Ireland, the UK, Germany, Greece, Spain, Italy, Poland, France and Austria.

Piper Sandler highlighted that the FreeNow acquisition should roughly double Lyft's (NASDAQ:LYFT) addressable market. Analyst Thomas Champion said the $197 million price tag for the European growth opportunity feels reasonable. He noted that around 50% of taxi bookings in Europe still occur offline, which gives the company a significant market share.

Separately, Oppenheimer started off coverage on Lyft (LYFT) on Wednesday with an Outperform rating. The firm expects the rideshare industry will continue to challenge the rising cost of car ownership, sustaining double-digit growth for multiple marketplaces. Robotaxi technology's ability to lower fares over time is seen representing further upside. Crucially, analyst Chad Larkin and his team think that Lyft (LYFT) shares are already factoring in the tariff impact at their current levels.

Shares of Lyft (LYFT) were up 1.1% in mid-morning trading.

data.title: Former SEC Chair Gary Gensler sounds alarm on Trump’s tariff strategy
data.description:
Former SEC Chair Gary Gensler warned that President Donald Trump’s aggressive tariff strategy risks destabilizing U.S. markets and weakening diplomatic leverage with China, according to remarks made during a recent CNBC interview.

Gensler noted policy uncertainty has already caused “choppy” financial conditions due to policy uncertainty.

During the interview, he emphasized the importance of consistent diplomacy, recalling his bipartisan negotiations that secured a 50-page compliance agreement with Chinese regulators.

“We treated China with respect but with firmness,” he said, adding that Beijing had largely honored the deal during his tenure.

He also cautioned that abrupt policy shifts undermine credibility, noting Chinese officials “lean back and don’t engage” when facing inconsistency.

The ex-regulator criticized the administration’s “all of the above” trade approach as counterproductive, comparing it to a “self-inflicted injury” for markets and alliances. He told interviewers that China perceives time as its ally, observing “they think they can outweigh the volatility of the policy on the other side” during erratic negotiations.

In addition, he warned the strategy could exacerbate instability in Treasury markets and corporate investment plans.

On insider trading concerns, Gensler stressed the need for stricter ethics rules after reports of lawmakers trading around tariff announcements.

“It’s really critical that folks don’t trade on inside information,” he said, praising the SEC’s internal ban on individual stock trades as a model. He urged Congress to adopt similar restrictions to maintain public trust.

Gensler concluded that market turbulence and business hesitation will persist without coherent trade policies.

“Businesses are pulling back – they have to be much more careful about investments,” he noted, linking the uncertainty to weakened economic confidence.

data.title: Western Digital receives Overweight rating as Morgan Stanley assumes coverage
data.description:
Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) received an Overweight rating by Morgan Stanley as the investment firm assumes coverage of the stock.

Morgan Stanley also set a $46 price target.

"Investor sentiment on HDDs leans negative, WDC short interest is at a 12 month high, and WDC's tax-free spin of Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) came at a challenging time – the market is down 10% since the spin, and WDC has underperformed by 20 points," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Erik Woodring, in a Wednesday investor note. "Yet, this is historically when you want to get involved in a cyclical hardware stock – when expectations are low and valuation has positive asymmetry."

Western Digital completed the spinoff of its flash business segment Sandisk in February. Western Digital now focuses on the hard disk drive business.

"Not to mention, we take a fundamentally more positive view than the market on the sustainability of HDD demand, which has been called into question with the recent tariff policy volatility; understand why WDC's valuation discount vs. peer Seagate Technology (STX) exists today, but believe that consistent execution will help close the valuation gap over the next 12 months; and believe that de-levering the balance sheet and initiating a dividend over the next 6–12 months will be accretive to equity holders."

However, Morgan Stanley noted that tariff uncertainty creates a potential downside risk to the HDD cycle. Currently, HDDs are unburdened by import tariffs, but that could change.

Still, cloud service providers such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN) expect strong HDD procurement plans.

"Cloud computing remained a top 3 CIO priority in our 1Q25 CIO survey, even as CIOs noted caution in hardware spending," Woodring added. "And interestingly, while our latest cloud capex tracker currently points to overall capex decelerating to 31% Y/Y growth in CY25, when excluding estimated spend on GPU/ASIC's, non-AI cloud capex is expected to accelerate in 2025, to +27% Y/Y growth."

Western Digital is slated to release its third quarter fiscal 2025 financial results pre-market on April 30. A consensus estimate expects adjusted earnings per share of $1.12 on revenue of $2.61B.

data.title: PetVivo enters distribution agreement with Eq Especialidades
data.description:
• PetVivo Holdings (OTCPK:PETV) has entered into a definitive distribution agreement with Eq Especialidades.
  • Eq Especialidades will inventory, market and promote PetVivo’s veterinary medical device, Spryng with OsteoCushion technology, throughout Mexico.
  • By adding Eq Especialidades to our current distribution network, they will assist PetVivo in achieving significant exposure to and penetration in the Mexican animal health market.
  • "We believe this new relationship will benefit the veterinary industry, the veterinarians who provide medical care to their patients, the animals receiving Spryng as well as the owners benefiting from the enhanced medical outcomes of their horses and companion animals." said John Lai, CEO for PetVivo Holdings.
  • Eq Especialidades sales and marketing activities will commence on April 1, 2025.

data.title: Humana discloses setback in bid to challenge 2025 Medicare Star Ratings cut: Bloomberg
data.description:
Humana (NYSE:HUM) has failed in an administrative appeal submitted to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services in response to its 2025 Medicare Star Ratings cut, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing a legal filing from the firm.  

The company shares plunged in October after Humana (NYSE:HUM) disclosed that only 25% of its Medicare Advantage members are enrolled in its top-rated MA plans for 2025 compared to 94% a year ago.

In the legal filing submitted on Tuesday, the managed care firm said that it lost the administrative appeal in a decision expected to become final by Apr. 28 following a review by CMS administrator Mehmet Oz.

The company is challenging the Star Ratings decision in a separate lawsuit filed in federal court in the Northern District of Texas.

Despite the setback for the in-house appeal, Humana (NYSE:HUM) said that the CMS decision undermines one of the agency’s arguments related to whether the company has evaluated its administrative remedies before the legal fight.

data.title: Blackstone Q1 earnings: Focus on AUM inflows, private equity outlook
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) will report its first-quarter earnings on April 17, and investors will keep an eye on fee-related earnings and AUM growth.  

The consensus EPS estimate is $1.07 (+9.2% Y/Y) and the consensus revenue estimate is $2.72B.

"Blackstone is set to report Q1 2025 earnings with mixed signals; a strong asset base but numerous downward EPS revisions suggest a tough quarter," Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian said.

Despite recent upgrades and a solid dividend yield, Blackstone's high payout ratio and rich valuation pose risks amid cooling market conditions, Ostian said, adding, "Key metrics to watch include fee-related earnings, AUM inflows, and realized performance revenue, with management's outlook on private equity and real estate crucial."

The asset manager mainly focuses on 4 core areas of investment: real estate, private equity, credit & insurance, and hedge funds.

Earlier in April, the firm was upgraded by Citizens to Market Outperform. In March, UBS upgraded Blackstone (NYSE:BX) to Buy from Neutral as the stock's recent 27% slump provides an entry point for a "premier alts [alternatives] platform with structural and scale advantages at a reasonable valuation."

UBS also figures that headwinds to Blackstone's (BX) real estate business have diminished, allowing for potential fee-related performance revenue contributions as early as 2025.

Blackstone (BX) is also reportedly considering acquiring a small minority stake in the U.S. operations of TikTok.

"Blackstone's diversified private markets platform and leading position in the industry make it a compelling buy, especially given the current attractive valuation and 4.5% dividend yield," Seeking Alpha analyst Perseus Perspectives said.

"The firm’s fee-earning AUM provides a stable revenue floor, ensuring dependable earnings even during market downturns, with significant growth potential in the private wealth channel," Perseus Perspectives added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Jonathan Weber believes declining interest rates are boosting demand for alternative investments, benefiting Blackstone's inflows and overall performance.

Last quarter, the private equity giant delivered stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings, as assets under management climbed, bolstered by strong inflows and a 14% Y/Y increase in management and advisory fees.

Over the last 2 years, BX has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 25% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 0 upward revisions and 17 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 0 upward revisions and 4 downward.

Shares in the company were down 24.6% since the start of the year.

data.title: Cloudflare rises after upgrade at Mizuho
data.description:
Shares of Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) rose about 3% on Wednesday after Mizuho upgraded the stock to Outperform from Neutral citing growing AI traction and the company being poised for acceleration. 

The firm, however, cut the price target to $135 from $140 on the shares of the cloud security solutions provider.

The analysts said their recent checks on Cloudflare were more favorable than they expected, and included strengthening pipelines, large-deal activity, and growing traction with Workers AI.

More broadly, the analysts believe Cloudflare has a highly scalable architecture and a culture of strong innovation, and they are bullish on the AI inferencing opportunity.

Despite higher macro uncertainty, the analysts said, they have a little more confidence in the multi-year acceleration story at Cloudflare, and with the shares having declined 38% since mid-February (versus the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) and Nasdaq down mid/high teens), they find the risk/reward attractive.
